methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M last post money.

methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Elroy Air is a Series B-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by David Merrill and Clint Cope, currently valued at $400M post-money on $116M total capital raised. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector includes seven private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Manna Aero, Asylon Robotics among others) and no listed public comparables, limiting traditional peer benchmarking.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peer data in this narrow sector. Applying a 1.9x Series B-to-next-stage multiplier to the $400M current post-money valuation implies a next-stage entry value of $760M. This step-up multiplier reflects typical venture progression patterns but should be treated as illustrative rather than prescriptive given the immature public trading history in autonomous systems hardware.

The analysis supports a range of $646M to $874M, with $760M as the median point. Key uncertainties include: the absence of calibrated public market comparables to validate stage-step assumptions; dispersion in valuation outcomes across the seven private peers, which remain privately held and thus opaque; and sector-specific technical and regulatory execution risks inherent to frontier drone commercialization. Investors should weight this stage-based framework alongside proprietary diligence on product development, regulatory pathway clarity, and unit economics.
